member.name: Julia Lopez
member.synopsis: Julia Lopez is the Conservative MP for Hornchurch and Upminster , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017. She currently undertakes the role of Shadow Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.answerText: The United States is our close ally and tech partner, and we are committed to ensuring that bond delivers real benefits for hardworking people on both sides of the Atlantic.We continue to have ministerial-level discussions with the US administration on sc...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not hold information centrally on the procurement dates of individual chips used within the Isambard AI and Dawn AI Research Resource clusters.Hardware for both systems was procured by delivery partners as part of wider system procurem...

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether her department plans to publish a response to its call for evidence on the AI Growth Lab scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The AI Growth Lab would be a cross-economy AI sandbox which would enable responsible AI products and services to be deployed under close supervision in live markets. This will unlock cross-economy growth, drive trust and create a mechanism for dynamic, ev...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Training

writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to building a digitally skilled workforce to support long-term economic growth, drive innovation and expand individual opportunity. As part of this ambition, DSIT has launched the AI Skills Boost campaign to upskill 10 million ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Sovereign AI Fund: Public Appointments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what process was followed to appoint Suzanne Ashman as Managing Partner of the UK Sovereign AI Fund.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Managing Partner of the Sovereign AI Fund has been appointed following a three-month, competitive, five-stage recruitment process involving over 60 candidates. She was judged the strongest candidate on merit against the skills and experience required....
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Advanced Research and Invention Agency: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the proportion of funding awarded by the Advanced Research and Invention Agency to non UK based companies; and how such funding is expected to deliver be
writtenQuestions.answerText: ARIA was established by Parliament to independently fund high-risk, high-reward research. More than 80% of its funding goes to UK-based teams, and where funding goes abroad, it comes with conditions to ensure it delivers long-term benefits for the UK, suc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Energy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the impact of energy prices on the cost and competitiveness of operating AI infrastructure in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Energy prices are a critical factor in the cost and competitiveness of operating AI infrastructure in the UK, but they are one of several key considerations, alongside access to reliable grid capacity, a strong digital services market, a highly developed ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.answerText: We are committed to ensuring the UK is the leading adopter of AI in the G7, and the Government will look at how regulation, data and access to finance can support adoption including by working closely with sectoral AI Champions to identify new solutions. ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Sovereign AI Unit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, if she will publish details of any public data sets made accessible to technology firms with which the Government has signed partnership agreements or Memoranda of Understanding in rela
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Sovereign AI Fund has not made any public sector datasets accessible for technology firms under MoUs or partnership agreements to date.

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what the volume of compute capacity was at (i) the Isambard-AI and (ii) Dawn AI Research Resource clusters in July 2024, July 2025 and April 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are investing up to £2 billion in public compute infrastructure until 2030. This will deliver a new national supercomputer in Edinburgh and expand our AI Research Resource (AIRR) twentyfold by 2030. The volume of compute capacity at the AIRR’s two supe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Trade Agreements: USA
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, pursuant to the answer provided on 26 February 2026 to question 114130, what recent progress she has made on the establishment and membership of the ministerial-level working group.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The United States is our close ally and tech partner. We continue to have regular discussions with the US administration on science and technology. This includes discussions on future governance and delivery timelines for the joint initiatives under the T...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Patents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, when she expects to publish the outcome to the consultation entitled Standard Essential Patents, published on 15 July 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Intellectual Property Office is continuing to analyse responses to its consultation on Standard Essential Patents and will publish its response in due course.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
